method_fallback_trail.reason: theoretical formula always available; V_nom 11kV within 50-15000V range

label.label_required_per: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(H) — drawout switchgear ≥240V (INT: IEEE 1584 best practice)
NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(H) — LV switchgear ≥240V (INT: IEEE 1584 best practice)

non_compliance_flags.message: Both cascade nodes fell back to Lee 1982 (conservative upper bound). IEEE 1584:2018 coefficients for HCB 14300V and VCB 600V pending transcription from Phase A standards layer.

assumptions: fault-level intent consumed; per-node Ibf + X/R from it
db-layout-rollup intent consumed; equipment_type from it; electrode_config auto-inferred (HCB for drawout, VCB for LV switchgear)
t_clear_s engineer-declared (0.5s ACB delayed at MV; 0.3s ACB electronic at LV)
IE values are Lee 1982 conservative upper bounds — actual IE per IEEE 1584:2018 will be 2-5× lower when coefficients transcribed
Voltage-class crossing: MV node at 14300V (HCB) → LV node at 600V (VCB); shock-approach boundaries differ per voltage range
tool_call_pending: true on both nodes — runtime calc tool awaits DraftsMan runtime ship

rationale.chat_summary: INT industrial 11kV/400V MV→LV cascade via transformer. MV node HCB auto-inferred, LV node VCB. Both Lee 1982 fallback (IEEE 1584 coefficients pending). MV IE ≈ 14 cal/cm² → Cat 3 / AFB 2200mm. LV IE ≈ 8.5 → Cat 3 / AFB 1300mm. Shock-approach: MV uses Table 130.4(C)(a) 751V-15kV row; LV uses 151V-750V row. tool_call_pending both nodes.

sections.title: Input Ingestion
sections.summary: Consumed fault-level + db-layout-rollup intents; per-node Ibf, X/R, equipment_type, ocpd_type, t_clear extracted.

sections.title: Cascade Topology
sections.summary: 2-node cascade with voltage-class crossing: 11kV service_entrance → 400V feeder via TX-mediated step-down. Both AC. Parent-child tree: MV-SWB → MV-SWB.TX-1.LV-MSB.

sections.title: Jurisdictional Framing
sections.summary: INT — IEEE 1584:2018 de facto standard. Labels recommended as engineering best practice (not statutory like US OSHA).

decisions.label: INT jurisdiction applied
decisions.summary: IEEE 1584:2018 baseline + NFPA 70E for shock-approach reference

sections.title: Electrode Configuration Auto-Inference
sections.summary: MV-SWB 'drawout switchgear' → HCB (horizontal-center-bank configuration for drawout breakers). LV-MSB 'LV switchgear' → VCB (vertical-center-bank, standard for LV). Both auto-inferred from equipment_type; no override needed.

sections.title: Method Selection + Fallback Chain
sections.summary: Both nodes attempted IEEE 1584:2018 → skipped (coefficients pending for HCB 14300V and VCB 600V). IEEE 1584:2002 skipped (also pending). Lee 1982 applied as always-available conservative baseline. LEE_1982_FALLBACK_USED info-severity flag emitted.

decisions.label: Lee 1982 fallback both nodes
decisions.summary: IEEE 1584:2018 + 2002 coefficients null. Lee theoretical (conservative 2-5× upper bound) applied.

sections.title: Per-Node Arc-Flash Results + Voltage-Class Crossing
sections.summary: MV-SWB: 11kV HCB → IE 14 cal/cm² (Cat 3) / AFB 2200mm / Table 130.4(C)(a) 751V-15kV shock row. LV-MSB: 400V VCB → IE 8.5 cal/cm² (Cat 3) / AFB 1300mm / Table 130.4(C)(a) 151V-750V shock row. Voltage crossing creates two distinct shock-approach regimes.

sections.title: Label Recommendations
sections.summary: Both nodes label_recommended: true. INT context: IEEE 1584 best practice for industrial substations. Labels would be mandatory by NFPA 70E §130.5(H) if installed in US; recommended as leading practice for INT.

sections.title: Compliance + Tool-call Status
sections.summary: Compliant. VOLTAGE_CLASS_CROSSING flag emitted (informational). tool_call_pending on both nodes. When IEEE 1584:2018 coefficients transcribed, method auto-promotes and IE values become realistic empirical estimates.
